_readme: Provider registry for scripts/advice_eval.py — REVIEWED COPY (consumer tiers verified 2026-07-21 against vendor free-tier docs and current coverage; sources in the integration notes). MEASUREMENT INTENT: each provider's consumer_default is the study's average-consumer proxy — the model the FREE / default tier of that vendor's consumer product serves, not the strongest API model. API models are PROXIES for consumer products: no product system prompt, no product safety wrapper, no memory — document the gap via a hand-run manual_ui calibration subset (import-manual-responses). Consumer tiers drift: every record archives the provider's exact resolved model string per call, and the pre-registration freezes a copy of this file. pricing = [input, output] USD per Mtok, used only by the local spend-ceiling math (worst-case, cache-miss rates); the true bill is the provider's. key_env names a GitHub Actions secret — keys never live in either repo or any local file.

anthropic.consumer_proxy_note: VERIFIED 2026-07-21: claude.ai's free tier defaults to Claude Sonnet 5 (free/Pro default since 2026-07-01), with Haiku 4.5 selectable. haiku stays set here as the pilot's cost floor per owner decision 2026-07-21 — a deliberate mismatch with the free-tier default, recorded in the pre-registration; the consumer-proxy arm proper should run anthropic:claude-sonnet-5 as an override. No default_pricing on purpose: the script's conservative built-in fallback prices Anthropic calls for the ceiling reserve (deliberate, matches the example; the ceiling test pins this). UPDATE 2026-07-23 (owner-directed): the sonnet-5 arm now runs the full registered design as the true free-tier default; haiku records stand as the labeled cost-floor arm.

openai.consumer_proxy_note: PHASE 2 (2026-07-22, owner decision): routed via OpenRouter with the owner's prepaid key. ChatGPT's free tier serves GPT-5.5 Instant; OpenAI's chat-latest alias (the exact product-tuned Instant model) is NOT listed on OpenRouter, so openai/gpt-5.5 is the stated approximation - the same model family without the ChatGPT product tune. Recorded fidelity gap; a direct OPENAI_API_KEY pointing at chat-latest is the higher-fidelity upgrade path. Pricing = GPT-5.5 rate + ~5% aggregator margin, worst case for the ceiling math.
openai.fallback_note: mini fallback arm (owner-directed 2026-07-23): the free ChatGPT tier falls back to a mini-class model once a session exhausts its GPT-5.5 message quota, so the mini arm measures the OVERFLOW experience of a non-paying user - labeled as the fallback arm, never as the default. SLUG CORRECTION (first fire, 2026-07-23): openai/gpt-5.5-mini does NOT exist on OpenRouter (400 Bad Request, run 30011607927, $0 spent); the arm runs openai/gpt-5.4-mini, the newest mini generation OpenRouter lists (verified via OpenRouter's public model pages). The exact mini variant the consumer product falls back to is unpublished and public sources conflict - recorded approximation; the manual_ui calibration subset measures the gap. Pricing = gpt-5.4-mini list ($0.75/$4.50) + ~5% markup + margin.

google.consumer_proxy_note: VERIFIED 2026-07-21: the free Gemini app defaults to Gemini 3.5 Flash (consumer default since 2026-05-19, Google I/O). The free tier also carries a small daily allowance of 3.1 Pro for hard questions — the Flash default is the average-consumer path. OpenAI-compatible endpoint path confirmed. min_interval_seconds=10 (2026-07-22): the AI Studio free tier throttled run 1b after 16 calls with sustained 429s that outlast retry backoff - pace to ~6 requests/min including retry attempts.

xai.consumer_proxy_note: PHASE 2 (2026-07-22): via OpenRouter (verified slug x-ai/grok-4.3, $1.25/$2.50 vendor rate + ~5%). The free grok.com / X surface serves limited Grok 4.3 access (~10 prompts per 2 h); same model id as the consumer default, vendor backend serves it.

deepseek.consumer_proxy_note: PHASE 2 (2026-07-22): via OpenRouter (verified slug deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash). Free app serves the V4 generation; the Expert/DeepThink toggle maps to the pro/reasoner - a second, NON-default arm. Flash chosen as the conservative non-reasoning consumer default; sources conflict on flash- vs pro-class for the web default - revisit at the calibration subset. Pricing = vendor rate + ~5%.

moonshot.consumer_proxy_note: PHASE 2 (2026-07-22): via OpenRouter (slug moonshotai/kimi-k2.5, $0.60/$3.00 vendor rate + ~5%). Free kimi.com serves a lighter variant than the paid K2.6 default; k2.5 is the closest match and the exact free-tier variant is unpublished - recorded approximation; the manual_ui calibration subset measures the gap.

openrouter.consumer_proxy_note: Route to any vendor's model with ONE prepaid key (a hard external spend ceiling on top of the script's). Use for Llama-family models (Meta AI's consumer app has no public API; an open-weights Llama via openrouter:meta-llama/... is a stated-and-recorded approximation, and true Meta AI behavior belongs in the manual_ui arm) or to consolidate vendors under one secret (~5% markup, an intermediary in the request path — record in the pre-registration if used). Always pass an explicit openrouter:<vendor/model> spec — no consumer_default on purpose.
openrouter.pricing_note: default_pricing is a GPT-tier catch-all for arbitrary openrouter:<vendor/model> specs. Flash-tier reroutes need a per-model entry or the ceiling meter bills them ~12x real and chokes the run mid-flight (2026-07-22: runs 1c and hedge-resume archived openrouter gemini records at the catch-all rate - archived cost_usd stands, overstatement documented in the pre-registration). Rates below: vendor list price + OpenRouter ~5% markup + margin.

copilot.consumer_proxy_note: No public API for the consumer Copilot product (re-checked 2026-07-21). Capture responses by hand in the real UI and import with: advice_eval.py import-manual-responses. Records land in the same audit chain with capture.method=manual_ui.

meta_ai.consumer_proxy_note: No public API for the consumer product; same manual_ui path as copilot. Open-weights Llama via openrouter is a separate, clearly-labeled approximation arm.

_thinking_machines_note: Re-checked 2026-07-21: still no consumer chat product — their shipped products are Tinker (fine-tuning API), the open-weights Inkling model (2026-07-15), and research-preview interaction models. No entry; re-check at the next revision.
_alias_vs_snapshot: Recorded measurement decision (2026-07-23, owner-directed review): each provider's registered target stays the CONSUMER DEFAULT - a rolling alias wherever that is what the vendor's free tier serves - because the study measures the product consumers get, not a frozen build. The served build is pinned per record instead: model_returned plus build_fingerprint (body-level, e.g. system_fingerprint) plus request_id from response headers, captured from 2026-07-23 forward. Switching any arm to a dated snapshot id is an access-mode change and must be recorded in the pre-registration before the affected fire.
